children.name: CmdScan
children.help: Looks for Windows Command History lists

children.name: Crashinfo
children.help: Lists the information from a Windows crash dump.

children.name: Info
children.help: Show OS & kernel details of the memory sample being analyzed.

children.name: PsXView
children.help: Lists all processes found via four of the methods described in "The Art of Memory Forensics" which may help identify processes that are trying to hide themselves.

children.name: pslist
help: This simple plugin is following the EPROCESS doubly-linked list
to get a list of all running process.
Note: This does not show unlinked/hidden processes.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.pslist.PsList [-h] [--physical] [--pid [PID ...]]

options:
-h, --help       show this help message and exit
--physical       Display physical offsets instead of virtual
--pid [PID ...]  Process ID to include

children.name: PsTree
children.help: Plugin for listing processes in a tree based on their parent process ID.

children.name: PsXView
children.help:
Lists all processes found via four of the methods described in "The Art of Memory Forensics" which may help identify processes that are trying to hide themselves.
This plugin is deprecated, use windows.malware.psxview.PsXView instead

children.name: PsScan
children.help: Scans for processes present in a particular windows memory image.
